text: Slides are about 60% done. Need to add the case vignette for session 3.
Karen — please upload mid-semester eval forms for each student by Oct 30.
Contract signed and returned. Honorarium W-9 submitted.
Will have all five eval forms uploaded by Oct 28. Marcus is co-signing for the two students on his ADHD/substance-use rotation.
Reminder the Week 6 reading list needs to be posted to Canvas before the Friday section. Let me know if you want me to mirror it on the program site.
Adding a discussion prompt on intergenerational trauma here — want students to connect it to the CFI domains we covered in Week 1.
Booked the Three reservation in Miami Beach for the night before the panel. Driving down, no flight needed.
Karen, I logged the FNA and NAMI honoraria in QuickBooks. Still waiting on the Broward Forum invoice — I'll follow up with their coordinator this week.
Thanks Danielle. Let's aim to keep at least two open slots in November for any APNA-referred invitations.
Strong framing on health equity. Consider opening with the Broward access-gap data — it really landed when you presented it at the Coalition.
Great suggestion, Lydia. I'll lead with the Broward numbers and tie them back to the APNA equity recommendations.
Two of the students are shadowing my medication-management clinic on Tuesdays. Both are doing well — happy to write a paragraph for their evals.
I can have my two students' competency checklists in by Oct 25. One needs another supervised intake before I sign off.
Coral Ridge Creative sent the revised CFI Pocket Guide cover. Approving the warm-sand palette over the teal version.
Mom — I set up the Bell Clinical Press storefront page so the three guides link to checkout. Want me to add the CME workbook when it's ready?
Linking the DSM-5-TR CFI handout from Bell Clinical Press here so students can download it directly.
If you have room on the panel, the NAMI Broward chapter would love a few minutes on local resources. Happy to make the intro.
NSU would be glad to co-sponsor your Cultural Competence CME session in the spring — could count toward student practicum hours too.
Headshot and bio were emailed to the FNA coordinator this morning. Read receipt confirmed.
Updating the population examples to reflect this year's cohort — adding Haitian Creole-speaking community resources from NAMI Broward.
Board Prep second edition: need to update the pharmacology questions to reflect the latest Aimovig and Synthroid dosing references before the next print run.
